name: storage
context: Upload, download, and manage objects in Google Cloud Storage buckets — lifecycle, signed URLs, and access control
info: This skill covers Google Cloud Storage (GCS) for object storage: creating and configuring buckets with location and storage class; uploading, downloading, and deleting objects; generating signed URLs for temporary access; configuring lifecycle rules for automatic deletion or class transitions; setting bucket-level and object-level ACLs or uniform bucket-level access; and enabling versioning for data protection. Operations use gsutil, gcloud storage commands, or the Python/Node.js client libraries. Agents use this skill to store application data, serve static assets, back up databases, and transfer files between services. See [[gcp/iam]] for storage-specific IAM roles (roles/storage.admin, roles/storage.objectViewer) and [[gcp/gcloud]] for CLI setup.
triggers: upload a file to cloud storage
copy files to google cloud storage
list objects in a gcs bucket
body:
# Google Cloud Storage

Google Cloud Storage (GCS) is an object storage service for any amount of data. Objects are stored in buckets, accessed via `gs://bucket-name/object-path` URIs. GCS provides four storage classes with different price/availability tradeoffs.

## Storage Classes

| Class | Min Duration | Use Case | Availability |
|-------|-------------|----------|--------------|
| Standard | None | Frequently accessed data | 99.99% |
| Nearline | 30 days | Monthly access | 99.95% |
| Coldline | 90 days | Quarterly access | 99.95% |
| Archive | 365 days | Yearly access / compliance | 99.95% |

## Creating Buckets

```bash
# Create a bucket (names are globally unique)
gcloud storage buckets create gs://my-app-data-prod \
  --location=us-central1 \
  --default-storage-class=standard \
  --uniform-bucket-level-access

# Create a multi-region bucket for high availability
gcloud storage buckets create gs://my-app-static \
  --location=us \
  --default-storage-class=standard

# List buckets in the project
gcloud storage buckets list

# Describe a bucket
gcloud storage buckets describe gs://my-app-data-prod
```

## Uploading and Downloading

```bash
# Upload a file
gcloud storage cp ./local-file.txt gs://my-bucket/path/file.txt

# Upload a directory recursively
gcloud storage cp -r ./build/ gs://my-bucket/static/

# Download a file
gcloud storage cp gs://my-bucket/path/file.txt ./local-file.txt

# Download a directory
gcloud storage cp -r gs://my-bucket/data/ ./local-data/

# Sync a directory (only copy changed files)
gcloud storage rsync -r ./local-dir/ gs://my-bucket/remote-dir/

# Sync with delete (mirror source to destination)
gcloud storage rsync -r -d ./local-dir/ gs://my-bucket/remote-dir/
```

## Listing and Managing Objects

```bash
# List objects in a bucket
gcloud storage ls gs://my-bucket/

# List with details (size, timestamp)
gcloud storage ls -l gs://my-bucket/path/

# Delete an object
gcloud storage rm gs://my-bucket/path/file.txt

# Delete all objects with a prefix
gcloud storage rm gs://my-bucket/temp/** --recursive

# Move/rename an object
gcloud storage mv gs://my-bucket/old-name.txt gs://my-bucket/new-name.txt

# Get object metadata
gcloud storage objects describe gs://my-bucket/path/file.txt
```

## Signed URLs

Generate time-limited URLs for unauthenticated access:

```bash
# Generate a signed URL valid for 1 hour (requires service account key)
gcloud storage sign-url gs://my-bucket/private/report.pdf \
  --private-key-file=sa-key.json \
  --duration=1h
```

```python
from google.cloud import storage
import datetime

def generate_signed_url(bucket_name: str, blob_name: str, expiration_minutes: int = 60) -> str:
    client = storage.Client()
    bucket = client.bucket(bucket_name)
    blob = bucket.blob(blob_name)
    url = blob.generate_signed_url(
        version="v4",
        expiration=datetime.timedelta(minutes=expiration_minutes),
        method="GET",
    )
    return url
```

## Lifecycle Rules

Automate object management with lifecycle policies:

```bash
# Set lifecycle via JSON config
cat > lifecycle.json << 'EOF'
{
  "rule": [
    {
      "action": {"type": "Delete"},
      "condition": {"age": 90}
    },
    {
      "action": {"type": "SetStorageClass", "storageClass": "NEARLINE"},
      "condition": {"age": 30, "matchesStorageClass": ["STANDARD"]}
    }
  ]
}
EOF

gcloud storage buckets update gs://my-bucket --lifecycle-file=lifecycle.json
```

## Public Access

```bash
# Make a bucket publicly readable (uniform access)
gcloud storage buckets add-iam-policy-binding gs://my-bucket \
  --member=allUsers \
  --role=roles/storage.objectViewer

# Make a single object public
gcloud storage objects update gs://my-bucket/public/index.html \
  --add-acl-grant=entity=allUsers,role=READER
```

## Python Client Library

```python
from google.cloud import storage

def upload_file(bucket_name: str, source_path: str, destination_blob: str) -> str:
    client = storage.Client()
    bucket = client.bucket(bucket_name)
    blob = bucket.blob(destination_blob)
    blob.upload_from_filename(source_path)
    return f"gs://{bucket_name}/{destination_blob}"

def download_file(bucket_name: str, source_blob: str, destination_path: str) -> None:
    client = storage.Client()
    bucket = client.bucket(bucket_name)
    blob = bucket.blob(source_blob)
    blob.download_to_filename(destination_path)

def list_blobs(bucket_name: str, prefix: str = "") -> list[str]:
    client = storage.Client()
    blobs = client.list_blobs(bucket_name, prefix=prefix)
    return [blob.name for blob in blobs]
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` | Default project ID |
| `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` | Path to service account key file |
| `CLOUDSDK_CORE_PROJECT` | Alternative project ID for gcloud |

sections.title: Bucket creation and configuration
sections.body: Create buckets with `gcloud storage buckets create gs://NAME --location=LOCATION --default-storage-class=CLASS`. Bucket names are globally unique across all GCP projects. Locations can be regional (`us-central1`), dual-region (`us-central1+us-east1`), or multi-region (`us`, `eu`, `asia`). Enable uniform bucket-level access (`--uniform-bucket-level-access`) to use IAM exclusively instead of ACLs. See [[gcp/iam]] for storage roles.

sections.title: Uploading and downloading objects
sections.body: Upload with `gcloud storage cp LOCAL gs://BUCKET/PATH`. Use `-r` for recursive directory uploads. Download with `gcloud storage cp gs://BUCKET/PATH LOCAL`. Sync directories with `gcloud storage rsync -r` (add `-d` to mirror deletions). For large files, gcloud auto-uses resumable uploads. Set Content-Type with `--content-type` or let it be auto-detected. Parallel uploads are default for multiple files.

sections.title: Signed URLs
sections.body: Generate time-limited URLs for unauthenticated access with `gcloud storage sign-url gs://BUCKET/OBJECT --private-key-file=KEY --duration=DURATION`. Via Python: `blob.generate_signed_url(version='v4', expiration=timedelta(hours=1), method='GET')`. Signed URLs work for GET (download), PUT (upload), and DELETE operations. Maximum duration is 7 days (v4 signing). Requires a service account key or impersonation.

sections.title: Lifecycle management
sections.body: Lifecycle rules automate object management: delete objects older than N days, transition storage classes based on age, delete non-current versions. Apply rules via `gcloud storage buckets update gs://BUCKET --lifecycle-file=FILE.json`. Rules evaluate daily. Combine with versioning for safe data retention — keep current versions in Standard, move non-current to Coldline after 30 days, delete after 365.

sections.title: Access control
sections.body: GCS supports two access models: **uniform bucket-level access** (recommended, IAM only) and **fine-grained** (ACLs per object). With uniform access, grant roles via `gcloud storage buckets add-iam-policy-binding`. Common roles: `roles/storage.objectViewer` (read), `roles/storage.objectCreator` (write), `roles/storage.admin` (full). For public websites, grant `allUsers` the objectViewer role. See [[gcp/iam]] for policy details.
